question: What are the practical limits on how many GB200 NVL72 racks can be deployed in a single data centre pod, and what are the constraining factors?

findings: The DGX GB200 NVL72 consumes 120 kW and weighs 1.36 metric tons (3,000 lbs); many data centres can only support a maximum of 60 kW per rack, making full deployment impossible in those facilities without infrastructure upgrades.
Eight DGX GB200 NVL72 racks can be networked together to form a DGX SuperPOD housing 576 GPUs; additional SuperPODs can be added for further scalability.

The GB200 NVL72 uses direct liquid cooling with coolant entering the rack at 25 °C and exiting 20 °C warmer, requiring in-rack or in-row CDU infrastructure; low-power peripherals are cooled with conventional 40 mm fans.

The GB200 NVL72 rack is equipped with six power shelves (three at top, three at bottom) to supply 120 kW; copper cabling is used internally instead of optical connections to reduce power draw by an additional 20 kW.
The GB200 NVL72 connects 36 Grace CPUs and 72 Blackwell GPUs in a rack-scale, liquid-cooled design with a 72-GPU NVLink domain acting as a single massive GPU.
